name: Apple Pie in Cheddar Crust
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the flour, cheese, and salt in a bowl. Using a pastry blender or two knives, cut in the shortening until coarse crumbs form.
2. Sprinkle iced water (about 5-6 tablespoons) into the mixture and using a fork, toss the crumbs until a dough is formed.
3. Press the dough firmly into a ball.
4. Divide the pastry almost in half and roll out the larger half on a lightly floured surface to a 13-inch circle.
5. Line a 9-inch pie plate with the pastry, trimming the edges to 1/2-inch beyond the rim of the pie plate.
6. Combine the apples, sugar, flour, and cinnamon in a bowl, mixing well. Arrange the apple mixture in the pastry-lined pie plate.
7. Roll out the remaining pastry to an 11-inch circle. Gently fold into quarters and cut steam slits in the folds.
8. Unfold the crust and place on top of the filling, trimming the crust to 1-inch beyond the rim of the pie plate.
9. Fold the top crust under the lower one and flute to form a ridge around the edge of the pie plate.
10. Combine the egg yolk and water, then brush the mixture over the top crust and rim.
11. Bake in a 400 degree F (200 C) oven for 45-50 minutes or until apples are tender and the crust is golden brown. Cool on a wire rack until slightly warm and serve with Vanilla Ice Cream, if desired.
